title: TC001-Guest vote attempt is gated and returns to feed for successful voting
description: Verifies a guest who attempts to vote is redirected to login, can authenticate, returns to the feed, and can successfully cast a vote with results displayed.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173/d:\\Govlyx\\frontend
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/d:\\Govlyx\\frontend")
        
        # -> Navigate to the application root (http://localhost:5173/) and wait for the SPA to load so interactive elements appear.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/")
        
        # -> Try loading the login route directly to see if the server responds (navigate to /login) and wait for the page to load.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., '1 vote')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The poll results should display the updated vote count after voting"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The feature could not be reached — the SPA did not load in the browser so the guest-vote flow could not be tested.

Observations:
- The page at http://localhost:5173/login is blank with 0 interactive elements.
- Attempts to load / and /login showed no UI and no interactive elements.
- I waited for the SPA to render but the page remained empty.

title: TC002-Unauthenticated user is redirected to login when visiting communities and returns after login
description: Verifies direct navigation to a protected route prompts login and, after authenticating, the user lands on the originally requested communities page with content loaded.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173/d:\\Govlyx\\frontend
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/d:\\Govlyx\\frontend")
        
        # -> Navigate directly to /communities to trigger the auth redirect and observe the login page.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/communities")
        
        # -> Fill the email and password fields, submit the login form, then verify the app redirects to /communities and that communities content is loaded.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Fill the email and password fields and submit the login form to authenticate, then wait for the app to redirect to /communities and load content.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[3]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Fill the email and password fields and submit the login form, then wait for the app to redirect to /communities and verify communities content loads.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[3]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Communities')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The communities list should be visible after logging in and redirecting to the communities page."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The login process did not complete and the app did not redirect to the protected /communities page, so the protected-route flow cannot be fully verified.

Observations:
- The login page shows 'Logging in...' but the URL remains on /login (no redirect to /communities).
- The email and password fields are present and pre-filled, but there is no active submit action completing the login.

title: TC003-Authenticated member can vote on a feed poll and sees immediate results
description: Verifies a logged-in member can vote on a poll from the home feed and immediately sees the poll results update.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173/d:\\Govlyx\\frontend
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/d:\\Govlyx\\frontend")
        
        # -> Load the app and open the login page (/login) so the login form and home feed can be reached.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login")
        
        # -> Fill the email and password fields and submit the login form to enter the app home feed.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Fill the email and password fields on the login form and submit the login button to reach the home feed.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[3]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Try to submit the login form again (use the password input and press Enter) to reach the home feed so a poll can be voted on.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'You voted')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The poll results should update immediately after the member votes in the home feed."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC005-Authenticated member can like and save a post on the feed and state persists on post detail

code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

        # Input text

title: TC007-Authenticated member can leave a community and see membership status revert
description: Verifies a logged-in member can join a community and then leave it, with the UI returning to a non-member state.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173/d:\\Govlyx\\frontend
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/d:\\Govlyx\\frontend")
        
        # -> Navigate to the application's login page so the SPA can load and show interactive elements (http://localhost:5173/login). Then wait for the page to render.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login")
        
        # -> Try allowing the SPA more time to initialize, then reload the root URL so the app can render its UI. If that fails, we'll report the feature as unreachable.

title: TC008-Authenticated member can save a post and see the saved state update
description: Verifies a logged-in member can save a post in the feed and the UI updates to reflect the saved state.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173/d:\\Govlyx\\frontend
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/d:\\Govlyx\\frontend")
        
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173/login and wait for the app to load, then re-check the page for interactive elements (login form or feed).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login")
        
        # -> Fill the email and password fields and submit the login form to sign in as the test user.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Fill the email and password fields with example@gmail.com / password123 and click 'Login to Portal' to sign in.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[3]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Saved')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The post should display 'Saved' after saving it in the feed."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC009-Authenticated member can share a post link via clipboard
description: Verifies a logged-in member can share a post from the feed and receives a confirmation that the link was copied.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173/d:\\Govlyx\\frontend
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/d:\\Govlyx\\frontend")
        
        # -> Navigate to the login page (/login) so the app can render the login form and interactive elements.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login")
        
        # -> Fill the email field with the test account email (example@gmail.com) as the immediate action.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Fill the email (index 131) and password (index 135) fields, then click the 'Login to Portal' button (index 208) to sign in.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[3]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[3]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Link copied to clipboard')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The page should show a confirmation that the link was copied after sharing a post"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
